title: Bug: dataset-backed accuracy evaluations process only 1 sample despite multi-row parquet datasets

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Implemented fix in CLI accuracy path: default --number-of-samples no longer caps dataset-backed runs unless explicitly provided. Added helper resolve_cloud_dataset_sample_limit + regression tests. Validation: (1) dataset-backed run without --number-of-samples logs 'Using 200 labeled samples' and 'Adjusted sample size from 200 to 200' for eval c84ce6b0-f388-45fd-be71-37e1b6df9d5b; (2) explicit cap still works, logs 'Using 50 labeled samples' and 'Adjusted sample size from 50 to 50' in acc_explicit50_fix.log.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Implemented fix: dataset-backed accuracy no longer applies implicit --number-of-samples default cap unless operator explicitly sets it. Added helper resolve_cloud_dataset_sample_limit() and tests. Runtime evidence from real eval log (/tmp/acc_full_dataset_fix.log): 'Using 200 labeled samples provided to AccuracyEvaluation' for evaluation c84ce6b0-f388-45fd-be71-37e1b6df9d5b (was 1 before).

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Validation: AW default dataset-backed run processed 200/200 with RCA present; explicit cap run processed 50/50 with RCA present. EDU logs confirm sample-count behavior (200 default, 50 explicit).

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Fixed Kanbus integrity blockers: repaired malformed plx-b092c7 JSON record and normalized 87 dependency targets from short/non-canonical IDs to full issue IDs via kbs dep remove/add replacements. kbs validate now passes.
